chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI. Cr. Bail Appl. No.518 of 2024 (Abdul Wahab Arshad vs. The State) Date Order with signature of Judge For hearing of Bail Application. 27.06.2024. Mr. Shah Imroz Khan, advocate for applicant Ms. Abida Parveen Channar, Special Prosecutor, ANF O R D E R MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: Allegedly when a suspicious parcel was attempted to be shipped to UK from Yaseenabad, Karachi through M/s. Logistic Shahra-e-Faisal Colony, Karachi. Anti-Narcotic Force (ANF) was duly notified by the courier service. The ANF Police reached there and on opening the parcel, it was found containing 24000 grams of heroin. 2. Applicant has been booked on the suspicion that he had given Rs.74,000/- to his sister Maheen, who had given that amount through easy paisa to one Faizan, who was Manager of Four Star Courier Service for its shipment. Neither Faizan, who had booked the parcel, nor Maheen, who gave him money to him are accused in this case. It is prima facie admitted that the applicant is neither a sender nor he had booked the parcel to be shipped to UK. He has been booked in this case on the ground that he had given Rs.7,400,000/- to his sister Maheen for shipping the parcel to UK. 3. Although, learned Special Prosecutor, ANF, has opposed bail but has not denied above facts. In the circumstances, the case against the applicant requires further inquiry and the role of applicant is yet to be determined in the trial. Resultantly, he is entitled to bail. Consequently, this bail application is allowed, and the applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.200,000/ (Rupees two hundred thousand only) case with P.R. bonds in the like amount to be executed to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 4.

Resultantly, he is entitled to bail. Consequently, this bail application is allowed, and the applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.200,000/ (Rupees two hundred thousand only) case with P.R. bonds in the like amount to be executed to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 4. Bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. JUDGE Rafiq/P.A
